title: The Nineteenth Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## October 11, 2020 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

---

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Nineteenth Sunday after Pentecost falls within Ordinary Time in the liturgical calendar. Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings for this date would be Job 23:1-9, 16-17 (or Amos 5:6-7, 10-15), Psalm 22:1-15, Hebrews 4:12-16, and Mark 10:17-31. This Sunday sits in the extended season after Pentecost, focusing on teaching about discipleship and faith through Jesus's encounter with the rich young ruler in the Gospel passage.

---

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was navigating its post-General Convention 2018 period with no major special sessions scheduled. Broderick Greer, who serves as canon at Saint John's, was increasingly visible as a vocal voice on racial justice issues within the denomination. The Diocese of Colorado was operating under Bishop Kimberly Lucas, who had been serving since 2014 and was active in diocesan reconciliation and social justice initiatives. No major contested elections or canonical crises were occurring in the Episcopal Church at this specific moment.

---

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was in the final weeks before the November 2020 election, with significant attention on Colorado's U.S. Senate race (Gardner vs. Hickenlooper) and local ballot initiatives. The city had experienced ongoing tension around pandemic-related restrictions and mask mandates, which remained contentious in conservative areas of Colorado. Downtown Denver and the Capitol Hill neighborhood near Saint John's Cathedral were dealing with encampments, homelessness issues, and post-protest recovery, following the summer's racial justice demonstrations. Weather in early October was typical fall conditions with cooler temperatures and some rain.

---

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS

The 2020 presidential election was in full campaign mode with just three weeks until Election Day; President Trump and Joe Biden were in heavy campaigning across swing states. The COVID-19 pandemic remained the dominant crisis, with over 210,000 American deaths recorded by this date and escalating debate over school reopenings and economic stimulus. The Senate confirmation hearings for Supreme Court Justice Amy Coney Barrett were scheduled to begin this same week (October 12), representing an extraordinarily contentious partisan moment. The racial justice movement continued amid ongoing tensions over policing and criminal justice reform.

---

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The COVID-19 pandemic was severely impacting Europe, with France, Spain, and the United Kingdom entering a second wave with rising case numbers and renewed restrictions. The U.S.-Taliban peace negotiations in Afghanistan remained stalled despite the February 2020 agreement, with violence continuing across the country. The ongoing crisis in Belarus following the contested August 2020 election saw continued protests and government crackdowns against demonstrators.

---

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

America was in a state of profound division and anxiety heading into the final stretch of an exceptionally polarized election cycle, with pervasive uncertainty about the legitimacy of the election process itself. The pandemic had created persistent social isolation and economic anxiety, with many churches still operating under capacity restrictions or hybrid worship models. The broader cultural moment was marked by mistrust in institutions, media fragmentation, and exhaustion from eight months of pandemic life compounded by ongoing racial justice reckoning. The Denver Broncos were in their early NFL season (2-4 record at this point), a minor distraction in an otherwise heavy news environment.
